results.scenarioName: AI discovery — llms.txt and machine-readable resources
results.prompt: Check https://loisirs74.fr/llms.txt and use what it declares to list the machine-readable resources this site offers to agents, then fetch one of those resources and extract one true fact from it. Read-only task: never submit forms or make bookings.

judge.check: Followed a resource actually declared in llms.txt (e.g. a content/*.md file or the API index)
Extracted a fact that is true per the fetched resource

content.text: I'll start by fetching the `llms.txt` file to see what it declares.

input.prompt: List verbatim all URLs and machine-readable resources (JSON, API endpoints, data files, feeds, etc.) declared in this file. Include the full URL and any description for each.

content.content:
# Loisirs 74: Complete URL and Machine-Readable Resource Inventory

## Core API & Data Endpoints

| Resource | URL | Description |
|----------|-----|-------------|
| **API Index** | `https://loisirs74.fr/api/lieux.json` | Machine-readable index of all 397 sites with slug, name, category, commune, GPS, and per-lieu facet URLs |
| **Full Content Dump** | `https://loisirs74.fr/llms-full.txt` | All 397 leisure destinations' French markdown concatenated for bulk ingestion |
| **Sitemap** | `https://loisirs74.fr/sitemap.xml` | Standard XML sitemap with lastmod, hreflang, and image entries |
| **AI Discovery Manifest** | `https://loisirs74.fr/.well-known/ai-info.json` | Discovery manifest listing all AI-related endpoints |

## Per-Lieu Content Patterns

**Canonical Pattern:** Substitute `{slug}` with destination identifier (e.g., `lac-blanc`):

| Format | URL Pattern | Language |
|--------|------------|----------|
| **Markdown (canonical)** | `https://loisirs74.fr/content/{slug}.md` | French |
| **Markdown (translated)** | `https://loisirs74.fr/content/en/{slug}.md` | English (12 languages available) |
| **Typed JSON** | `https://loisirs74.fr/api/lieu/{slug}.json` | Structured data with nulls preserved |
| **HTML (French)** | `https://loisirs74.fr/{slug}` | Web page |
| **HTML (multilingual)** | `https://loisirs74.fr/{lang}/{slug}` | Other language variants |

## Category Hub Markdown Files

| Category | URL | Count |
|----------|-----|-------|
| Lakes & Beaches | `https://loisirs74.fr/content/lacs.md` | 31 sites |
| Waterfalls | `https://loisirs74.fr/content/cascades.md` | 17 sites |
| Leisure Bases | `https://loisirs74.fr/content/bases-de-loisirs.md` | 33 sites |
| Viewpoints | `https://loisirs74.fr/content/points-de-vue.md` | 28 sites |
| Cable Cars | `https://loisirs74.fr/content/telecabines.md` | 8 sites |
| Castles | `https://loisirs74.fr/content/chateaux.md` | 26 sites |
| Trails | `https://loisirs74.fr/content/sentiers.md` | 40 sites |
| Greenways | `https://loisirs74.fr/content/voies-vertes.md` | 5 sites |
| Museums | `https://loisirs74.fr/content/musees.md` | 49 sites |
| Attractions | `https://loisirs74.fr/content/attractions.md` | 160 sites |

## Machine-readable resources declared in `loisirs74.fr/llms.txt`

**Core data endpoints (site-wide):**
| Resource | URL | Description |
|---|---|---|
| API index | `https://loisirs74.fr/api/lieux.json` | Index of all sites with slug, name, category, commune, GPS, and per-site facet URLs |
| Full content dump | `https://loisirs74.fr/llms-full.txt` | All destinations' French markdown concatenated for bulk ingestion |
| Sitemap | `https://loisirs74.fr/sitemap.xml` | XML sitemap with lastmod, hreflang, image entries |
| AI discovery manifest | `https://loisirs74.fr/.well-known/ai-info.json` | Manifest listing all AI-related endpoints |

**Per-site (substitute `{slug}` / `{lang}`):**
- `https://loisirs74.fr/content/{slug}.md` — canonical French markdown
- `https://loisirs74.fr/content/en/{slug}.md` — translated markdown (12 languages)
- `https://loisirs74.fr/api/lieu/{slug}.json` — typed JSON (nulls preserved)
- `https://loisirs74.fr/{slug}` and `https://loisirs74.fr/{lang}/{slug}` — HTML pages

**Category hub markdown files:** `lacs.md`, `cascades.md`, `bases-de-loisirs.md`, `points-de-vue.md`, `telecabines.md`, `chateaux.md`, `sentiers.md`, `voies-vertes.md`, `musees.md`, `attractions.md` (all under `/content/`).

## One verified fact
I fetched the API index (`/api/lieux.json`) and read its first record. **The Abbaye d'Aulps (slug `abbaye-d-aulps`) is located in the commune of Saint-Jean-d'Aulps, at GPS coordinates 46.2419, 6.6498.**

This was a read-only fetch — no forms submitted, no bookings made.

results.scenarioName: Comparison — two Lac d'Annecy beaches for small children
results.prompt: Using https://loisirs74.fr, compare two beaches on Lac d'Annecy on entry price and lifeguard supervision, then recommend one of the two for a family with small children and say why. Read-only task: browse and read pages only, never submit forms or make bookings.

judge.check: Compared two real beach pages that exist on loisirs74.fr
Price and lifeguard-supervision facts match the pages
Gave a reasoned recommendation grounded in those facts

content.text: I'll research beaches on Lac d'Annecy via loisirs74.fr. Let me load the web tools first.
Let me start by fetching the loisirs74.fr homepage to understand the site structure.

input.prompt: List all links and sections related to beaches (plages) on Lac d'Annecy. Provide the URLs and names of beach pages.

content.content:
# Lac d'Annecy Beaches

Based on the webpage content, here are the beaches listed for Lac d'Annecy:

## Beach Pages and URLs

1. **Plage de Saint-Jorioz**
   - URL: https://loisirs74.fr/plage-de-saint-jorioz
   - Description: "The only large natural sand beach on Lake Annecy, with shaded lawns, diving platform and accessible facilities"

2. **Plage d'Albigny**
   - URL: https://loisirs74.fr/plage-albigny
   - Description: "The largest public beach on Lake Annecy, with 400 meters of grassy shores facing the mountains"

3. **Plage des Marquisats**
   - URL: https://loisirs74.fr/plage-des-marquisats
   - Description: "A 200-meter grassy beach on the western shore, supervised in summer, with disability access"

All three beaches are marked as **Gratuit** (Free) and are located in the "Lacs & plages" (Lakes & Beaches) section of the guide. Saint-Jorioz offers the only natural sand option, while the Annecy locations provide grassy areas near the town center.

input.prompt: What is the entry price (tarif/prix d'entrée) and is there lifeguard supervision (surveillance/maître-nageur/baignade surveillée)? Also note facilities relevant to families with small children (shallow water, sand, shade, accessibility). Quote exact details.

What is the entry price (tarif/prix d'entrée) and is there lifeguard supervision (surveillance/maître-nageur/baignade surveillée)? Also note facilities relevant to families with small children (shallow water, sand, shade, accessibility). Quote exact details.

content.content:
# Plage de Saint-Jorioz: Pricing, Supervision & Family Facilities

## Entry Prices
**July-August (High Season):**
- Adults (18+): €2.60
- Children (10-17): €1.00
- Under 10: Free
- After 16:30: €1.00 (all ages)
- Season pass: €22.00

**September-June:** Free access

## Lifeguard Supervision
"Baignade surveillée par des maîtres-nageurs de 9h30 à 17h30 en juillet-août" (Lifeguards present 9:30 AM-5:30 PM during July-August only). No supervision outside peak season.

## Family-Friendly Features
- **Shallow water:** "Plage de sable avec pente douce, idéale pour les familles" (sand beach with gentle slope ideal for families)
- **Sand:** "la seule grande plage de sable naturel du lac d'Annecy" (only large natural sand beach on Lake Annecy)
- **Shade:** "vastes pelouses ombragées" (extensive shaded lawns)
- **Paddling area:** Pataugeoire (children's wading pool)
- **Play area:** "aire de jeux pour les plus jeunes" (playground for young children)
- **Accessibility:** Wheelchair ramps, amphibious chairs free in July-August, level entry

content.content:
# Plage d'Albigny: Entry Price, Lifeguard Supervision & Family Facilities

**Entry Price:**
"Gratuit" (Free). The beach offers "Accès libre · Gratuit" year-round with no admission fee.

**Lifeguard Supervision:**
"Surveillance assurée par des maîtres-nageurs en juillet-août de 11h à 19h" (Lifeguards on duty July-August, 11am-7pm). Outside this period, swimming is unsupervised.

**Family-Friendly Features:**

- **Shallow water & gentle slope:** "Le fond du lac est sablonneux avec une pente douce facilitant l'accès à l'eau pour les jeunes enfants"
- **Sand & grassy areas:** 400+ meters of "rives engazonnées et sablonneuses"
- **Shade:** "Grande pelouse ombragée par endroits avec bosquets"
- **Accessibility:** "Rampe de mise à l'eau PMR. Fauteuils flottants gratuits disponibles en juillet-août"
- **Facilities:** Three free restroom blocks, outdoor showers, children's play areas, and changing facilities

content.text:
I browsed loisirs74.fr and read the two beach pages. Here's the comparison.

## Comparison: Two beaches on Lac d'Annecy

| | **Plage de Saint-Jorioz** | **Plage d'Albigny** |
|---|---|---|
| **Entry price** | Paid in July–Aug: adults €2.60, children 10–17 €1.00, **under 10 free**, €1.00 after 16:30, season pass €22. Free Sept–June. | **Free year-round** ("Accès libre · Gratuit") |
| **Lifeguard supervision** | Yes, July–Aug only, **9:30–17:30** ("baignade surveillée par des maîtres-nageurs") | Yes, July–Aug only, **11:00–19:00** |
| **Beach type** | The only large **natural sand** beach on the lake, gentle slope | Mostly grassy shores; sandy bottom with gentle slope into the water |
| **Kid facilities** | Wading pool (pataugeoire), playground, shaded lawns | Play areas, shaded lawn in spots, showers/restrooms |
| **Accessibility** | Wheelchair ramps, free amphibious chairs (Jul–Aug), level entry | PMR launch ramp, free floating chairs (Jul–Aug) |

## Recommendation: **Plage de Saint-Jorioz**

For a family with small children, I'd choose **Saint-Jorioz**, despite the modest summer fee, because:

- **Real sand + gentle slope** — it's the only large natural sand beach on the lake, with a "pente douce, idéale pour les familles." Sand is far more comfortable for toddlers to play and crawl on than Albigny's mainly grassy shore.
- **Dedicated toddler features** — a **wading pool (pataugeoire)** and a **playground for young children**, which Albigny doesn't advertise.
- **Longer morning coverage** — lifeguards start at **9:30** vs. 11:00 at Albigny, matching the earlier rhythm of families with little ones (and both close their supervised swimming in the afternoon/evening).
- **Cost is minimal** — children under 10 are free and adults are only €2.60 (or €1.00 after 16:30), so the price barely offsets the family-oriented advantages.

Albigny is the better pick if a completely free, larger, town-adjacent grassy space is the priority — but on the two criteria that matter most for small kids (sandy shallow entry + toddler amenities), Saint-Jorioz is the stronger choice.

*Note: I only browsed and read pages — no forms submitted or bookings made. Prices and supervision hours are as listed on loisirs74.fr for the July–August 2026 season; worth reconfirming on-site as seasonal details can change.*

## Recommendation: **Plage de Saint-Jorioz**

- **Real sand + gentle slope** — it's the only large natural sand beach on the lake, with a "pente douce, idéale pour les familles." Sand is far more comfortable for toddlers to play and crawl on than Albigny's mainly grassy shore.
- **Dedicated toddler features** — a **wading pool (pataugeoire)** and a **playground for young children**, which Albigny doesn't advertise.
- **Longer morning coverage** — lifeguards start at **9:30** vs. 11:00 at Albigny, matching the earlier rhythm of families with little ones (and both close their supervised swimming in the afternoon/evening).
- **Cost is minimal** — children under 10 are free and adults are only €2.60 (or €1.00 after 16:30), so the price barely offsets the family-oriented advantages.

Albigny is the better pick if a completely free, larger, town-adjacent grassy space is the priority — but on the two criteria that matter most for small kids (sandy shallow entry + toddler amenities), Saint-Jorioz is the stronger choice.

*Note: I only browsed and read pages — no forms submitted or bookings made. Prices and supervision hours are as listed on loisirs74.fr for the July–August 2026 season; worth reconfirming on-site as seasonal details can change.*

# Lac d'Annecy Beaches

Based on the webpage content, here are the beaches listed for Lac d'Annecy:

## Beach Pages and URLs

1. **Plage de Saint-Jorioz**
   - URL: https://loisirs74.fr/plage-de-saint-jorioz
   - Description: "The only large natural sand beach on Lake Annecy, with shaded lawns, diving platform and accessible facilities"

2. **Plage d'Albigny**
   - URL: https://loisirs74.fr/plage-albigny
   - Description: "The largest public beach on Lake Annecy, with 400 meters of grassy shores facing the mountains"

3. **Plage des Marquisats**
   - URL: https://loisirs74.fr/plage-des-marquisats
   - Description: "A 200-meter grassy beach on the western shore, supervised in summer, with disability access"

All three beaches are marked as **Gratuit** (Free) and are located in the "Lacs & plages" (Lakes & Beaches) section of the guide. Saint-Jorioz offers the only natural sand option, while the Annecy locations provide grassy areas near the town center.

entries.toolResultText:
# Plage de Saint-Jorioz: Pricing, Supervision & Family Facilities

## Entry Prices
**July-August (High Season):**
- Adults (18+): €2.60
- Children (10-17): €1.00
- Under 10: Free
- After 16:30: €1.00 (all ages)
- Season pass: €22.00

**September-June:** Free access

## Lifeguard Supervision
"Baignade surveillée par des maîtres-nageurs de 9h30 à 17h30 en juillet-août" (Lifeguards present 9:30 AM-5:30 PM during July-August only). No supervision outside peak season.

## Family-Friendly Features
- **Shallow water:** "Plage de sable avec pente douce, idéale pour les familles" (sand beach with gentle slope ideal for families)
- **Sand:** "la seule grande plage de sable naturel du lac d'Annecy" (only large natural sand beach on Lake Annecy)
- **Shade:** "vastes pelouses ombragées" (extensive shaded lawns)
- **Paddling area:** Pataugeoire (children's wading pool)
- **Play area:** "aire de jeux pour les plus jeunes" (playground for young children)
- **Accessibility:** Wheelchair ramps, amphibious chairs free in July-August, level entry

# Plage d'Albigny: Entry Price, Lifeguard Supervision & Family Facilities

**Entry Price:**
"Gratuit" (Free). The beach offers "Accès libre · Gratuit" year-round with no admission fee.

**Lifeguard Supervision:**
"Surveillance assurée par des maîtres-nageurs en juillet-août de 11h à 19h" (Lifeguards on duty July-August, 11am-7pm). Outside this period, swimming is unsupervised.

**Family-Friendly Features:**

- **Shallow water & gentle slope:** "Le fond du lac est sablonneux avec une pente douce facilitant l'accès à l'eau pour les jeunes enfants"
- **Sand & grassy areas:** 400+ meters of "rives engazonnées et sablonneuses"
- **Shade:** "Grande pelouse ombragée par endroits avec bosquets"

entries.text:
I browsed loisirs74.fr and read the two beach pages. Here's the comparison.

## Comparison: Two beaches on Lac d'Annecy

| | **Plage de Saint-Jorioz** | **Plage d'Albigny** |
|---|---|---|
| **Entry price** | Paid in July–Aug: adults €2.60, children 10–17 €1.00, **under 10 free**, €1.00 after 16:30, season pass €22. Free Sept–June. | **Free year-round** ("Accès libre · Gratuit") |
| **Lifeguard supervision** | Yes, July–Aug only, **9:30–17:30** ("baignade surveillée par des maîtres-nageurs") | Yes, July–Aug only, **11:00–19:00** |
| **Beach type** | The only large **natural sand** beach on the lake, gentle slope | Mostly grassy shores; sandy bottom with gentle slope into the water |
| **Kid facilities** | Wading pool (pataugeoire), playground, shaded lawns | Play areas, shaded lawn in spots, showers/restrooms |
| **Accessibility** | Wheelchair ramps, free amphibious chairs (Jul–Aug), level entry | PMR launch ramp, free floating chairs (Jul–Aug) |

## Recommendation: **Plage de Saint-Jorioz**

For a family with small children, I'd choose **Saint-Jorioz**, despite the modest summer fee, because:

- **Real sand + gentle slope** — it's the only large natural sand beach on the lake, with a "pente douce, idéale pour les familles." Sand is far more comfortable for toddlers to play and crawl on than Albigny's mainly grassy shore.
- **Dedicated toddler features** — a **wading pool (pataugeoire)** and a **playground for young children**, which Albigny doesn't advertise.
- **Longer morning coverage** — lifeguards start at **9:30** vs. 11:00 at Albigny, matching the earlier rhythm of families with little ones (and both close their supervised swimming in the afternoon/evening).
- **Cost is minimal** — children under 10 are free and adults are only €2.60 (or €1.00 after 16:30), so the price barely offsets the family-oriented advantages.

Albigny is the better pick if a completely free, larger, town-adjacent grassy space is the priority — but on the two criteria that matter most for small kids (sandy shallow entry + toddler amenities), Saint-Jorioz is the stronger choice.

*Note: I only browsed and read pages — no forms submitted or bookings made. Prices and supervision hours are as listed on loisirs74.fr for the July–August 2026 season; worth reconfirming on-site as seasonal details can change.*

Here's what I found about the **Plage de Saint-Jorioz** on loisirs74.fr:

## Overview
The Plage de Saint-Jorioz is the only large natural sandy beach on Lake Annecy. Located on the western shore, with views of the Dents de Lanfon and the Tournette, it extends over about 2,200 m² and combines a fine sandy beach (about 100 meters long) with expansive shaded meadows.

## Opening Times & Access
The municipal beach is open year-round: free and open access from September to June, paid and supervised access from July 1 to August 31 from 9:30 AM to 5:30 PM.

More specifically:
- January 1 – June 30: Daily free access.
- July 1 – August 31: Paid access from 9:30 AM to 5:30 PM (swimming supervised by lifeguards). Closed from 7:00 to 9:30 AM for cleaning.
- September 1 – December 31: Daily free access.

## Pricing (2026)
Adult (18 years and older): €2.60 · Child 10–17 years: €1 · Under 10 years: free · Entry after 4:30 PM: €1 · Season ticket (unlimited access July–August): €22 · Chip card support (processing fee): €2. Off-season (Sept.–June): free.

## Ticketing
Online ticketing at saint-jorioz.fr (E-Shop): purchase of chip card, 10–20 pack, or single ticket (QR code by email). Purchase is also possible at the beach ticket offices or at the town hall.

## Labels & Accessibility
Awarded the Blue Flag label since 2010, the beach guarantees high environmental standards: daily cleaning during the season, sand renewal every four years, waste management, and public awareness. It has also received the Tourism and Disability label thanks to special facilities: water access and free provision of two amphibious wheelchairs (Tiralo and Hippocampe) in July–August.

## Practical Info
- **Dogs:** Dogs are prohibited year-round at the Plage de Saint-Jorioz facility, even on a leash, in accordance with the Blue Flag label.
- **Parking:** The Plage de Saint-Jorioz has a large free shaded parking lot in front of the entrance. In summer it is often full: arrive before 11:00 AM or use the alternative parking areas (marina, sports complex).

*Note: The information was confirmed on the official website on May 14, 2026, subject to seasonal changes.*

content.text:
Here are the three facts for **Plage de Saint-Jorioz**, from [loisirs74.fr](https://loisirs74.fr/plage-de-saint-jorioz):

**1. Entry price** — Paid access applies only in summer (1 July–31 August); free the rest of the year.
- Adult (18+): €2.60
- Child 10–17: €1
- Under 10: free
- Entry after 4:30 PM: €1
- (Season pass: €22)

**2. Opening hours** — Open year-round.
- 1 July – 31 August: paid, lifeguard-supervised access from **9:30 AM to 5:30 PM**
- September through June: free, open access

**3. Wheelchair accessibility** — Accessible, with the *Tourisme & Handicap* label. Facilities include a ramp to the water, a beach mat with handrail, adapted restrooms, and **two free amphibious wheelchairs (Tiralo + Hippocampe)** available in July–August.

Source: [Plage de Saint-Jorioz — Loisirs 74](https://loisirs74.fr/plage-de-saint-jorioz)

(As requested, this was read-only — I only browsed and read pages, and submitted no forms or bookings.)

## Overview
The Plage de Saint-Jorioz is the only large natural sandy beach on Lake Annecy. Located on the western shore, with views of the Dents de Lanfon and the Tournette, it extends over about 2,200 m² and combines a fine sandy beach (about 100 meters long) with expansive shaded meadows.

## Opening Times & Access
The municipal beach is open year-round: free and open access from September to June, paid and supervised access from July 1 to August 31 from 9:30 AM to 5:30 PM.

More specifically:
- January 1 – June 30: Daily free access.
- July 1 – August 31: Paid access from 9:30 AM to 5:30 PM (swimming supervised by lifeguards). Closed from 7:00 to 9:30 AM for cleaning.
- September 1 – December 31: Daily free access.

## Pricing (2026)
Adult (18 years and older): €2.60 · Child 10–17 years: €1 · Under 10 years: free · Entry after 4:30 PM: €1 · Season ticket (unlimited access July–August): €22 · Chip card support (processing fee): €2. Off-season (Sept.–June): free.

## Ticketing
Online ticketing at saint-jorioz.fr (E-Shop): purchase of chip card, 10–20 pack, or single ticket (QR code by email). Purchase is also possible at the beach ticket offices or at the town hall.

## Labels & Accessibility
Awarded the Blue Flag label since 2010, the beach guarantees high environmental standards: daily cleaning during the season, sand renewal every four years, waste management, and public awareness. It has also received the Tourism and Disability label thanks to special facilities: water access and free provision of two amphibious wheelchairs (Tiralo and Hippocampe) in July–August.

## Practical Info
- **Dogs:** Dogs are prohibited year-round at the Plage de Saint-Jorioz facility, even on a leash, in accordance with the Blue Flag label.
- **Parking:** The Plage de Saint-Jorioz has a large free shaded parking lot in front of the entrance. In summer it is often full: arrive before 11:00 AM or use the alternative parking areas (marina, sports complex).

*Note: The information was confirmed on the official website on May 14, 2026, subject to seasonal changes.*"],"durationSeconds":15.60090301,"searchCount":1}}

Here are the three facts for **Plage de Saint-Jorioz**, from [loisirs74.fr](https://loisirs74.fr/plage-de-saint-jorioz):

**1. Entry price** — Paid access applies only in summer (1 July–31 August); free the rest of the year.
- Adult (18+): €2.60
- Child 10–17: €1
- Under 10: free
- Entry after 4:30 PM: €1
- (Season pass: €22)

**2. Opening hours** — Open year-round.
- 1 July – 31 August: paid, lifeguard-supervised access from **9:30 AM to 5:30 PM**
- September through June: free, open access

**3. Wheelchair accessibility** — Accessible, with the *Tourisme & Handicap* label. Facilities include a ramp to the water, a beach mat with handrail, adapted restrooms, and **two free amphibious wheelchairs (Tiralo + Hippocampe)** available in July–August.

Source: [Plage de Saint-Jorioz — Loisirs 74](https://loisirs74.fr/plage-de-saint-jorioz)

Source: [Plage de Saint-Jorioz — Loisirs 74](https://loisirs74.fr/plage-de-saint-jorioz)

# Patinoire Jean Régis, Annecy

**Description:**
"Patinoire couverte de 58 × 28 m gérée par la ville d'Annecy" featuring public skating sessions, Friday disco evenings, and courses in figure skating and hockey.

**Location/Address:**
27 rue du Stade, 74000 Annecy (near Parc des Sports)

**Opening Hours:**
- Wednesday, Saturday, Sunday: 14:00–18:00
- Friday: 20:00–23:00 (disco night)
- Daily during school holidays
- Open year-round

**Prices (2026):**
- Adult: €4.60 + skate rental €3.40
- Children: €3.00
- Seasonal passes available

**Session Duration:** 2 hours

**Accessibility:** Yes (PMR/wheelchair accessible); free onsite parking available

# Le Bowl Bowling Annecy

**Description:**
"16 PISTES + RESTAURANT + NIGHTCLUB - complexe loisirs nocturne d'Annecy ville." This venue offers bowling combined with dining and nightclub facilities, marketed as a complete evening experience in one location.

**Location/Address:**
9 Boulevard du Fier, 74000 Annecy (central axis connecting the old town to commercial areas)

**Opening Hours:**
- Monday-Thursday: 2 PM–1 AM
- Friday-Saturday: 2 PM–4 AM
- Sunday: 2 PM–midnight

**Prices (2026):**
- Adults: €8 per game
- Children: €6 per game
- Family packages available for evenings
- Average duration: 30-45 minutes per game

**Additional Details:**
Free onsite parking; PMR-accessible ground floor; children's birthday parties available; Sunday tournaments offered.

- Children/students: Reduced rates available

- Bar/snack available
- Passeport escalade required for autonomous climbing on routes, or initial training session needed
- ~1-3 hours average visit duration

content.text:
I have enough to build the plan. Here are three solid indoor venues, all sourced from **loisirs74.fr**.

## 🌧️ Rainy Afternoon in Annecy — Family Indoor Plan

### 1. 🎳 Le Bowl Bowling — Annecy *(easy, all-ages start)*
- **Where:** 9 Boulevard du Fier, 74000 Annecy (central, free on-site parking)
- **What:** 16-lane bowling complex with restaurant on site — a relaxed, low-effort activity everyone can play together
- **Hours:** Mon–Thu 2 PM–1 AM; Fri–Sat 2 PM–4 AM; Sun 2 PM–midnight
- **Prices:** **€8/game adult**, **€6/game child** (~30–45 min per game); family evening packages available
- Ground floor is PMR-accessible

### 2. ⛸️ Patinoire Jean Régis — Annecy *(covered ice rink)*
- **Where:** 27 rue du Stade, 74000 Annecy (near Parc des Sports, free parking)
- **What:** Covered 58×28 m ice rink with public skating sessions — good rainy-day energy-burner for kids
- **Hours (public sessions):** Wed/Sat/Sun 2 PM–6 PM (open daily during school holidays) — a 2-hour afternoon session fits perfectly
- **Prices:** **Adult €4.60** + skate rental **€3.40**; **Children €3.00**
- Wheelchair accessible

### 3. 🧗 Climb Up Annecy — Indoor Climbing *(for older kids/teens)*
- **Where:** Zone industrielle de Seynod/Vovray, 74000 Annecy (free parking, bar/snack on site)
- **What:** Large indoor climbing gym — routes up to 18 m, bouldering area with thick mats, auto-belay systems, gear rental
- **Hours:** Open daily, extended evening/weekend hours
- **Prices:** **Adult entry ~€13–16**; reduced rates for children/students; 10-entry passes available
- Note: autonomous route climbing needs a "passeport escalade" or an intro session — best suited to bouldering/auto-belay for a casual family visit

### Suggested flow
Start with **bowling** early afternoon (indoors, low-key, lunch on site) → move to the **ice rink** for the 2 PM–6 PM public session, or swap in **Climb Up** if your kids are older and more active.

### Rough budget (family of 4 — 2 adults, 2 children)
- Bowling (1 game each): 2×€8 + 2×€6 = **€28**
- Ice rink (with 2 adult skate rentals): 2×(€4.60+€3.40) + 2×€3.00 = **€22**
- Climb Up instead of one of the above: ~€13–16 × adults + reduced child rates

---
**Source:** All details and prices from [loisirs74.fr](https://loisirs74.fr) (2026 tariffs listed on the site). I only browsed and read pages — no forms submitted, no bookings made. A couple of specifics (Climb Up's exact address/hours, family package details) were flagged "to confirm" on the site itself, so I'd verify those on each venue's official page before you go.

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-8","id":"msg_013XVUG67QMuVm6J4z1T3oC7","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"text","text":"I have enough to build the plan. Here are three solid indoor venues, all sourced from **loisirs74.fr**.

## 🌧️ Rainy Afternoon in Annecy — Family Indoor Plan

### 1. 🎳 Le Bowl Bowling — Annecy *(easy, all-ages start)*
- **Where:** 9 Boulevard du Fier, 74000 Annecy (central, free on-site parking)
- **What:** 16-lane bowling complex with restaurant on site — a relaxed, low-effort activity everyone can play together
- **Hours:** Mon–Thu 2 PM–1 AM; Fri–Sat 2 PM–4 AM; Sun 2 PM–midnight
- **Prices:** **€8/game adult**, **€6/game child** (~30–45 min per game); family evening packages available
- Ground floor is PMR-accessible

### 2. ⛸️ Patinoire Jean Régis — Annecy *(covered ice rink)*
- **Where:** 27 rue du Stade, 74000 Annecy (near Parc des Sports, free parking)
- **What:** Covered 58×28 m ice rink with public skating sessions — good rainy-day energy-burner for kids
- **Hours (public sessions):** Wed/Sat/Sun 2 PM–6 PM (open daily during school holidays) — a 2-hour afternoon session fits perfectly
- **Prices:** **Adult €4.60** + skate rental **€3.40**; **Children €3.00**
- Wheelchair accessible

### 3. 🧗 Climb Up Annecy — Indoor Climbing *(for older kids/teens)*
- **Where:** Zone industrielle de Seynod/Vovray, 74000 Annecy (free parking, bar/snack on site)
- **What:** Large indoor climbing gym — routes up to 18 m, bouldering area with thick mats, auto-belay systems, gear rental
- **Hours:** Open daily, extended evening/weekend hours
- **Prices:** **Adult entry ~€13–16**; reduced rates for children/students; 10-entry passes available
- Note: autonomous route climbing needs a "passeport escalade" or an intro session — best suited to bouldering/auto-belay for a casual family visit

### Suggested flow
Start with **bowling** early afternoon (indoors, low-key, lunch on site) → move to the **ice rink** for the 2 PM–6 PM public session, or swap in **Climb Up** if your kids are older and more active.

### Rough budget (family of 4 — 2 adults, 2 children)
- Bowling (1 game each): 2×€8 + 2×€6 = **€28**
- Ice rink (with 2 adult skate rentals): 2×(€4.60+€3.40) + 2×€3.00 = **€22**
- Climb Up instead of one of the above: ~€13–16 × adults + reduced child rates

---
**Source:** All details and prices from [loisirs74.fr](https://loisirs74.fr) (2026 tariffs listed on the site). I only browsed and read pages — no forms submitted, no bookings made. # Indoor Activities & Family-Friendly Venues Near Annecy

## Sport & Games
- **Le Bowl Bowling Annecy** - "16 PISTES + RESTAURANT + NIGHTCLUB" bowling complex in Annecy city
- **Patinoire Jean Régis** - Indoor ice rink with "SÉANCES PUBLIQUES + SOIRÉE DISCO VENDREDI" and skating lessons
- **Atome Climbing** - Indoor climbing gym in downtown Annecy
- **Climb Up Annecy** - Indoor climbing wall facility with "PLUS DE 200 VOIES"

## Water Activities
- **Centre Aquatique Sallanches Mont-Blanc** - Covered aquatic center with pool, activity basin, and wellness area in nearby Sallanches

## Entertainment & Wellness
- **Casino Impérial Palace Annecy** - Lakeside casino with hotel and restaurant
- **Pathé Annecy** - Multiplex cinema with approximately 8 screens

## Kids' Activities
- **Criq'Parc** - Tree-based play park for ages 3-12 on Lake Annecy shores at Saint-Jorioz (just outside Annecy)
- **Parc animalier de la Grande Jeanne** - Free municipal wildlife park accessible year-round

## Workshops
- **Atelier de poterie du Prunier** (Thônes) - Pottery classes for children and adults

[Full listings at https://loisirs74.fr/](https://loisirs74.fr/)

# Patinoire Jean Régis, Annecy

entries.toolResultText:
# Le Bowl Bowling Annecy

**Description:**
"16 PISTES + RESTAURANT + NIGHTCLUB - complexe loisirs nocturne d'Annecy ville." This venue offers bowling combined with dining and nightclub facilities, marketed as a complete evening experience in one location.

**Location/Address:**
9 Boulevard du Fier, 74000 Annecy (central axis connecting the old town to commercial areas)

**Opening Hours:**
- Monday-Thursday: 2 PM–1 AM
- Friday-Saturday: 2 PM–4 AM
- Sunday: 2 PM–midnight

**Prices (2026):**
- Adults: €8 per game
- Children: €6 per game
- Family packages available for evenings
- Average duration: 30-45 minutes per game

**Additional Details:**
Free onsite parking; PMR-accessible ground floor; children's birthday parties available; Sunday tournaments offered.

# Climb Up Annecy - Climbing Gym

**Description:**
"Grande salle d'escalade indoor" operated by the Climb Up national network, featuring climbing routes up to 18 meters, a bouldering area with thick mats, training wall, auto-belay systems, and equipment rental. Courses and group activities available.

**Location/Address:**
Zone industrielle de Seynod/Vovray, 74000 Annecy (to be confirmed)
GPS: ~45.892° N · ~6.108° E

**Opening Hours:**
"Ouvert tous les jours, horaires étendus le soir et le week-end" (Open daily with extended evening/weekend hours). Specific times listed as "to confirm on official site."

**Prices (2026):**
- Adult entry: "~13-16 €"
- Children/students: Reduced rates available
- Subscriptions and 10-entry passes offered
- Equipment rental included with entry
- Public transit (SIBRA): €1.50-€2.50

**Additional Details:**
- Free on-site parking
- Bar/snack available
- Passeport escalade required for autonomous climbing on routes, or initial training session needed
- ~1-3 hours average visit duration

entries.text:
I have enough to build the plan. Here are three solid indoor venues, all sourced from **loisirs74.fr**.

## 🌧️ Rainy Afternoon in Annecy — Family Indoor Plan

### 1. 🎳 Le Bowl Bowling — Annecy *(easy, all-ages start)*
- **Where:** 9 Boulevard du Fier, 74000 Annecy (central, free on-site parking)
- **What:** 16-lane bowling complex with restaurant on site — a relaxed, low-effort activity everyone can play together
- **Hours:** Mon–Thu 2 PM–1 AM; Fri–Sat 2 PM–4 AM; Sun 2 PM–midnight
- **Prices:** **€8/game adult**, **€6/game child** (~30–45 min per game); family evening packages available
- Ground floor is PMR-accessible

### 2. ⛸️ Patinoire Jean Régis — Annecy *(covered ice rink)*
- **Where:** 27 rue du Stade, 74000 Annecy (near Parc des Sports, free parking)
- **What:** Covered 58×28 m ice rink with public skating sessions — good rainy-day energy-burner for kids
- **Hours (public sessions):** Wed/Sat/Sun 2 PM–6 PM (open daily during school holidays) — a 2-hour afternoon session fits perfectly
- **Prices:** **Adult €4.60** + skate rental **€3.40**; **Children €3.00**
- Wheelchair accessible

### 3. 🧗 Climb Up Annecy — Indoor Climbing *(for older kids/teens)*
- **Where:** Zone industrielle de Seynod/Vovray, 74000 Annecy (free parking, bar/snack on site)
- **What:** Large indoor climbing gym — routes up to 18 m, bouldering area with thick mats, auto-belay systems, gear rental
- **Hours:** Open daily, extended evening/weekend hours
- **Prices:** **Adult entry ~€13–16**; reduced rates for children/students; 10-entry passes available
- Note: autonomous route climbing needs a "passeport escalade" or an intro session — best suited to bouldering/auto-belay for a casual family visit

### Suggested flow
Start with **bowling** early afternoon (indoors, low-key, lunch on site) → move to the **ice rink** for the 2 PM–6 PM public session, or swap in **Climb Up** if your kids are older and more active.

### Rough budget (family of 4 — 2 adults, 2 children)
- Bowling (1 game each): 2×€8 + 2×€6 = **€28**
- Ice rink (with 2 adult skate rentals): 2×(€4.60+€3.40) + 2×€3.00 = **€22**
- Climb Up instead of one of the above: ~€13–16 × adults + reduced child rates

---
**Source:** All details and prices from [loisirs74.fr](https://loisirs74.fr) (2026 tariffs listed on the site). I only browsed and read pages — no forms submitted, no bookings made. A couple of specifics (Climb Up's exact address/hours, family package details) were flagged "to confirm" on the site itself, so I'd verify those on each venue's official page before you go.

criteria.check: Named 3 real venues that exist as pages on loisirs74.fr
criteria.rationale: All three named venues — Le Bowl Bowling Annecy, Patinoire Jean Régis, and Climb Up Annecy — exist as real pages on loisirs74.fr. I independently fetched each URL and all returned valid venue pages, confirming they are genuine, not hallucinated.

criteria.check: Prices quoted match what the venue pages state
criteria.rationale: Prices match the venue pages exactly on independent verification: Bowling €8 adult / €6 child per game; Patinoire €4.60 adult + €3.40 skate rental, €3.00 child; Climb Up ~€13–16 adult with reduced/10-entry passes. The agent also appropriately flagged Climb Up's pricing as 'to confirm' as the page itself states.

criteria.check: Used the site's hub/category navigation to discover venues
criteria.rationale: The agent discovered venues only from the homepage overview, then guessed venue URLs (two 404s) before re-fetching the homepage for exact hrefs. It never used the site's actual hub/category navigation (15 categories like 'Sport & jeux' or 'Parcs, jardins & familles') to browse and discover indoor venues. Discovery was homepage + URL-guessing, not category-hub navigation.

audits.rationale: WebFetch to the homepage completed successfully, returning a well-formed, structured list of venues and categories in 5.2s.

Service responded correctly but the request hit HTTP 404 Not Found (agent-guessed URL that does not exist). The server behaved reliably and surfaced the error clearly, but the call did not return the requested content.
Same as #11 — clean HTTP 404 returned for a nonexistent guessed URL. Service functioned correctly but the call returned an error status with no content.
WebFetch to the Patinoire page completed and returned complete, well-formed venue data (description, address, hours, prices) in 3.4s.
WebFetch to the homepage returned a well-formed list of exact venue href URLs, enabling correction of the earlier bad guesses.
WebFetch to the corrected bowling URL completed successfully, returning full structured venue and pricing data in 3.8s.
WebFetch to the corrected Climb Up URL completed successfully, returning full structured venue and pricing data in 9.3s (slow but valid).
System/infrastructure message, no agent decision to fault.
System/infrastructure message.
System/infrastructure message.
Clear, focused opening plan: states intent to research via loisirs74.fr and load web tools.
Necessary tool discovery — loads WebFetch/WebSearch schemas via a precise select query. Right-sized.
Sound plan to start from the homepage to understand site structure before diving into venues.
Well-scoped homepage fetch with a targeted prompt; returned a rich, directly usable venue list that seeded the whole plan.
System/infrastructure message.
System/infrastructure message.
Reasonable plan to fetch venue detail pages in parallel for pricing.
Tool ran but hit 404 — agent guessed the URL slug rather than reading exact hrefs from the homepage first. Output was unusable; caused rework.
Tool ran but 404 from another guessed slug. Unusable output; part of the wrong-URL retry pattern.
Guessed slug happened to be correct; returned full description, hours, and 2026 prices for the ice rink — used directly in the final plan.
System/infrastructure message.
System/infrastructure message.
Good self-correction: recognizes the two 404s stemmed from guessed URLs and decides to retrieve exact links.
Effective recovery fetch requesting exact hrefs; returned correct URLs that unblocked the remaining venues. This is the step that should have preceded #11/#12.
Concise plan to re-fetch bowling and climbing with the corrected URLs.
Well-scoped fetch returned full bowling details and per-game prices used in the final plan and budget.
Targeted fetch returned climbing description, hours, and price range; used in the plan with appropriate 'to confirm' caveats.
System/infrastructure message.
System/infrastructure message.
Strong synthesis: three indoor venues with quoted prices, hours, a suggested flow, family budget, and honest sourcing/caveats. Respected the read-only constraint (no forms/bookings).

necessity.rationale: Nearly all interactions were necessary and on-task. #11 and #12 were avoidable: the agent guessed venue URL slugs instead of first extracting exact hrefs from the homepage (which it already had), producing two 404s and requiring the recovery fetch #17. The extra homepage read #17 was itself only needed because of those wrong guesses. Everything else — tool discovery, homepage survey, and the successful venue fetches — was warranted for the task.

I searched for "loisirs74.fr fiche lieu activité" but wasn't able to find results from that specific domain. The search results returned related sites about leisure and activities in Haute-Savoie (department 74) instead. Here's what I found:

## Sites de loisirs et activités en Haute-Savoie (74)

**Sites touristiques officiels**
- Le site Haute-Savoie Tourisme regroupe les loisirs culturels, sportifs et ludiques en Haute Savoie, pour occuper votre temps libre en pratiquant des activités sportives ou culturelles dans la région savoyarde. On y trouve des complexes de loisirs, lieux où se mêlent de nombreuses activités, ainsi que des lieux où pratiquer une activité sportive avec les stades, les stations de ski, les piscines et patinoires.

**Plateformes de réservation d'activités**
- **Funbooker** : Avec Funbooker, vous pouvez découvrir les meilleures activités à faire en Haute-Savoie, en choisissant parmi un choix varié d'expériences pour créer des souvenirs, en famille ou entre amis, avec un meilleur prix garanti et une annulation gratuite.
- **Cap Adrénaline** : propose de nombreuses activités à sensations, notamment du saut en parachute avec vue sur les sommets enneigés aux sauts à l'élastique au cœur des gorges alpines.

**Carte de réductions loisirs (LCE74)**
- LCE74 propose des loisirs de proximité ou sur toute la France pour satisfaire les envies de tous, avec de nombreux partenaires au choix. La Carte Loisirs ouvre l'intégralité de leur offre de loisirs et de services à prix réduit, et inclut également une assurance pour la pratique de vos loisirs.

---

**Note :** Le site spécifique **loisirs74.fr** ne figure pas dans les résultats de recherche. Si vous cherchez une "fiche lieu" ou "fiche activité" précise sur ce domaine, il serait utile de me préciser :
- Le nom du lieu ou de l'activité recherché(e), ou
- La ville concernée en Haute-Savoie

Je pourrais alors affiner la recherche. Voulez-vous que je relance une recherche plus ciblée ?

content.text: WebFetch strips `<script>` tags during markdown conversion, so it can't see JSON-LD. Let me fetch the raw HTML directly.

input.description: Fetch raw HTML of a fiche page

content.content:
HTTP status / size:
92373 /tmp/fiche.html

input.command: cd /tmp; grep -o '<script[^>]*application/ld+json[^>]*>' fiche.html; echo "---count of ld+json blocks---"; grep -c 'application/ld+json' fiche.html

content.content:
<script type="application/ld+json">
---count of ld+json blocks---
1
Shell cwd was reset to /tmp/axis-nQWIhB/work

input.command:
cd /tmp && python3 - <<'EOF'
import re
html=open('fiche.html',encoding='utf-8').read()
blocks=re.findall(r'<script type="application/ld\+json">(.*?)</script>',html,re.S)
print("JSON-LD blocks:",len(blocks))
for b in blocks:
    print(b.strip())
print("\n--- microdata itemtype occurrences ---")
for m in re.findall(r'itemtype="[^"]*"',html): print(m)
print("\n--- lat/long/geo mentions ---")
for m in re.findall(r'.{0,40}(?:latitude|longitude|geo|GeoCoordinates)[^\n]{0,60}',html,re.I)[:20]: print(m.strip())
EOF

content.text:
I fetched a live fiche page (`Château de Menthon-Saint-Bernard`) and pulled the raw HTML directly, since `WebFetch`'s markdown conversion drops `<script>` tags where the structured data lives.

## Schema.org structured data exposed

The page carries **one JSON-LD block** (`<script type="application/ld+json">`) using a `@graph` array — no microdata or RDFa. The graph contains four typed nodes:

| `@type` | Purpose | Key properties |
|---|---|---|
| **WebSite** | Site identity | `url`, `name` ("Loisirs 74"), `inLanguage` fr-FR |
| **BreadcrumbList** | Navigation trail | Accueil → Châteaux → Château de Menthon-Saint-Bernard |
| **TouristAttraction** | The place itself | `name`, `alternateName`, `description`, `address` (PostalAddress), `geo` (GeoCoordinates), `amenityFeature` (6× LocationFeatureSpecification), `offers` (Offer, 12 € EUR), `isAccessibleForFree: false`, `publicAccess: true` |
| **FAQPage** | 8 Q&A pairs | `mainEntity` of Question/Answer (réservation, tarifs, horaires, accessibilité PMR, durée, parking, etc.) |

The `<head>` also carries legacy **geo meta tags** mirroring the same location: `geo.region=FR-74`, `geo.placename=Menthon-Saint-Bernard`, `geo.position=45.8647;6.2097`.

## GPS coordinates

From `TouristAttraction.geo` (`@type: GeoCoordinates`):

- **Latitude: `45.8647`**
- **Longitude: `6.2097`**

```json
"geo": {"@type": "GeoCoordinates", "latitude": 45.8647, "longitude": 6.2097}
```

These match the `geo.position` meta tag (`45.8647;6.2097`), placing it on the east shore of Lac d'Annecy in Haute-Savoie. This was a read-only fetch — no forms submitted, no `offers`/billetterie links followed.

## Sites de loisirs et activités en Haute-Savoie (74)

**Sites touristiques officiels**
- Le site Haute-Savoie Tourisme regroupe les loisirs culturels, sportifs et ludiques en Haute Savoie, pour occuper votre temps libre en pratiquant des activités sportives ou culturelles dans la région savoyarde. On y trouve des complexes de loisirs, lieux où se mêlent de nombreuses activités, ainsi que des lieux où pratiquer une activité sportive avec les stades, les stations de ski, les piscines et patinoires.

**Plateformes de réservation d'activités**
- **Funbooker** : Avec Funbooker, vous pouvez découvrir les meilleures activités à faire en Haute-Savoie, en choisissant parmi un choix varié d'expériences pour créer des souvenirs, en famille ou entre amis, avec un meilleur prix garanti et une annulation gratuite.
- **Cap Adrénaline** : propose de nombreuses activités à sensations, notamment du saut en parachute avec vue sur les sommets enneigés aux sauts à l'élastique au cœur des gorges alpines.

**Carte de réductions loisirs (LCE74)**
- LCE74 propose des loisirs de proximité ou sur toute la France pour satisfaire les envies de tous, avec de nombreux partenaires au choix. La Carte Loisirs ouvre l'intégralité de leur offre de loisirs et de services à prix réduit, et inclut également une assurance pour la pratique de vos loisirs.

## Sites de loisirs et activités en Haute-Savoie (74)

**Sites touristiques officiels**
- Le site Haute-Savoie Tourisme regroupe les loisirs culturels, sportifs et ludiques en Haute Savoie, pour occuper votre temps libre en pratiquant des activités sportives ou culturelles dans la région savoyarde. On y trouve des complexes de loisirs, lieux où se mêlent de nombreuses activités, ainsi que des lieux où pratiquer une activité sportive avec les stades, les stations de ski, les piscines et patinoires.

**Plateformes de réservation d'activités**
- **Funbooker** : Avec Funbooker, vous pouvez découvrir les meilleures activités à faire en Haute-Savoie, en choisissant parmi un choix varié d'expériences pour créer des souvenirs, en famille ou entre amis, avec un meilleur prix garanti et une annulation gratuite.
- **Cap Adrénaline** : propose de nombreuses activités à sensations, notamment du saut en parachute avec vue sur les sommets enneigés aux sauts à l'élastique au cœur des gorges alpines.

**Carte de réductions loisirs (LCE74)**
- LCE74 propose des loisirs de proximité ou sur toute la France pour satisfaire les envies de tous, avec de nombreux partenaires au choix. La Carte Loisirs ouvre l'intégralité de leur offre de loisirs et de services à prix réduit, et inclut également une assurance pour la pratique de vos loisirs.

toolInput.command:
cd /tmp && python3 - <<'EOF'
import re
html=open('fiche.html',encoding='utf-8').read()
blocks=re.findall(r'<script type="application/ld\+json">(.*?)</script>',html,re.S)
print("JSON-LD blocks:",len(blocks))
for b in blocks:
    print(b.strip())
print("\n--- microdata itemtype occurrences ---")
for m in re.findall(r'itemtype="[^"]*"',html): print(m)
print("\n--- lat/long/geo mentions ---")
for m in re.findall(r'.{0,40}(?:latitude|longitude|geo|GeoCoordinates)[^\n]{0,60}',html,re.I)[:20]: print(m.strip())
EOF

entries.text:
I fetched a live fiche page (`Château de Menthon-Saint-Bernard`) and pulled the raw HTML directly, since `WebFetch`'s markdown conversion drops `<script>` tags where the structured data lives.

## Schema.org structured data exposed

The page carries **one JSON-LD block** (`<script type="application/ld+json">`) using a `@graph` array — no microdata or RDFa. The graph contains four typed nodes:

| `@type` | Purpose | Key properties |
|---|---|---|
| **WebSite** | Site identity | `url`, `name` ("Loisirs 74"), `inLanguage` fr-FR |
| **BreadcrumbList** | Navigation trail | Accueil → Châteaux → Château de Menthon-Saint-Bernard |
| **TouristAttraction** | The place itself | `name`, `alternateName`, `description`, `address` (PostalAddress), `geo` (GeoCoordinates), `amenityFeature` (6× LocationFeatureSpecification), `offers` (Offer, 12 € EUR), `isAccessibleForFree: false`, `publicAccess: true` |
| **FAQPage** | 8 Q&A pairs | `mainEntity` of Question/Answer (réservation, tarifs, horaires, accessibilité PMR, durée, parking, etc.) |

The `<head>` also carries legacy **geo meta tags** mirroring the same location: `geo.region=FR-74`, `geo.placename=Menthon-Saint-Bernard`, `geo.position=45.8647;6.2097`.

## GPS coordinates

From `TouristAttraction.geo` (`@type: GeoCoordinates`):

- **Latitude: `45.8647`**
- **Longitude: `6.2097`**

```json
"geo": {"@type": "GeoCoordinates", "latitude": 45.8647, "longitude": 6.2097}
```

These match the `geo.position` meta tag (`45.8647;6.2097`), placing it on the east shore of Lac d'Annecy in Haute-Savoie. This was a read-only fetch — no forms submitted, no `offers`/billetterie links followed.

criteria.check: Identified the JSON-LD @type(s) present on the fiche
Extracted GPS coordinates that match the page's JSON-LD geo data

audits.rationale: curl completed successfully, fetched 92373 bytes to /tmp/fiche.html, and wc -c reported the size cleanly. No errors.
grep ran to completion, found the ld+json script tag and reported count=1. The cwd-reset note is a harness housekeeping message, not a command failure; exit was clean.
python3 heredoc executed without error, parsed the HTML file, and printed JSON-LD blocks, microdata, and geo matches as intended.
WebFetch on homepage completed cleanly, returned a well-formed 4.2KB markdown list of fiche URLs in 14.2s. No errors.
WebSearch executed successfully and returned well-formed results (3.3KB) with links, even though the specific domain wasn't indexed. The service itself responded reliably.

WebFetch on the fiche page completed and returned a coherent structured-data analysis. The absence of JSON-LD in the output stems from WebFetch stripping <script> tags during markdown conversion — a tool characteristic, not a service failure; the call itself succeeded.
curl fetched the raw HTML successfully (92373 bytes, exit 0, 166ms). Server responded with full page content, no network or HTTP errors.
System init, required infrastructure.
System init, required infrastructure.
System init, required infrastructure.
Thinking/planning step, productive lead-in to tool discovery.
Tool discovery for WebFetch/WebSearch — necessary since these are deferred tools; tiny, focused query.
Thinking step planning the fetch approach.
WebFetch of homepage to enumerate fiche URLs — correct approach, returned a rich, directly usable list of detail pages.
WebSearch was redundant: #7 already yielded plenty of fiche URLs. It returned off-domain noise (unrelated tourism sites) that the agent did not use. Ran successfully but added no value.
System step.
System step.
System step.
Thinking step selecting a specific fiche to analyze.

WebFetch of the chosen fiche with a well-crafted schema.org extraction prompt. Output was a false negative (WebFetch strips scripts) but the agent correctly diagnosed this and used it as the pivot to a better method — so the result was informative.
System step.
System step.
Excellent self-correction: correctly reasoned that WebFetch's markdown conversion drops <script> JSON-LD and switched to raw HTML fetch.
curl of raw HTML to a temp file with size check — right-sized, exactly the right tool to recover the stripped script tags.
Focused grep confirming presence/count of ld+json blocks before full extraction — proportionate and useful.
Thinking step planning the extraction script.
Python script extracting JSON-LD, microdata, and geo mentions in one pass — recovered the full structured data including GeoCoordinates. Directly delivered the answer.
Final report: accurate, well-structured summary of @types, GPS coords cross-checked against geo meta tags, and read-only compliance noted. Fully satisfies the task.

necessity.rationale: Nearly all interactions were necessary and well-chained: discover tools → enumerate fiches → attempt WebFetch → diagnose script-stripping → recover raw HTML → extract JSON-LD. The only unnecessary call was the WebSearch (#8), which was redundant given #7 already provided fiche URLs and returned only off-domain noise the agent ignored.
